- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
Copilot left **one review** on PR #38, with **no inline comments** but one suppressed low-confidence note worth addressing:

**Concern (ContactManagerApp.swift:132):** In `CONTACTMANAGER_UI_TEST_MODE` the app still builds a CloudKit-backed container (`cloudKitDatabase: .automatic`). Combined with the forced seeding, a developer running UI tests while signed into iCloud could push the sample contacts to their CloudKit DB and sync them across devices. Copilot suggests forcing a local-only configuration (CloudKit disabled) in UI-test mode.

That's a legitimate point — the seed-on-launch behavior makes the CloudKit risk real. Want me to fix it (force `cloudKitDatabase: .none` when `CONTACTMANAGER_UI_TEST_MODE` is set)?

96	    // MARK: - Container loading
97	
98	    private static func loadContainer(resettingStore: Bool = false) -> ContainerLoadState {
99	        // UI test mode is detected via env var and pre-empts the unit-test
100	        // guard below: XCUITest injects an automation-support library
101	        // that drags `XCTestCase` into the app's loaded classes, so the
102	        // `NSClassFromString` check would short-circuit to `.testing` and
103	        // render `EmptyView()`. UI tests run against an in-memory,
104	        // CloudKit-disabled container so they never touch the user's real
105	        // on-disk store or push sample contacts into iCloud.
106	        let isUITestMode = ProcessInfo.processInfo
107	            .environment["CONTACTMANAGER_UI_TEST_MODE"] != nil
108	
109	        if !isUITestMode {
110	            // When hosting the unit tests, skip building the app's model
111	            // container entirely so the test target owns the only
112	            // container in the process.
113	            let isUnitTesting = ProcessInfo.processInfo
114	                .environment["XCTestConfigurationFilePath"] != nil
115	                || NSClassFromString("XCTestCase") != nil
116	            if isUnitTesting { return .testing }
117	        }
118	
119	        if resettingStore {
120	            deleteDefaultStore()
121	        }
122	
123	        let schema = Schema([Contact.self, ContactField.self, ContactGroup.self])
124	
125	        // UI-test mode uses an in-memory, CloudKit-disabled container so
126	        // tests are deterministic and can't sync sample contacts to a
127	        // signed-in iCloud account or pollute the user's local store.
128	        if isUITestMode {
129	            do {
130	                let configuration = ModelConfiguration(
131	                    schema: schema,
132	                    isStoredInMemoryOnly: true,
133	                    cloudKitDatabase: .none
134	                )
135	                let container = try ModelContainer(for: schema, configurations: configuration)
136	                do {
137	                    try SampleData.seedIfNeeded(container.mainContext)
138	                } catch {
139	                    print("ContactManager: sample data seeding skipped — \(error)")
140	                }
141	                EntityModelContainer.shared = container
142	                return .ready(container)
143	            } catch {
144	                return .failed(error.localizedDescription)
145	            }
146	        }
147	
148	        // Build a CloudKit-backed container when the iCloud capability is
149	        // present, and fall back to a local-only container otherwise so the
150	        // app still runs without iCloud.
151	        let container: ModelContainer
152	        var isLocalOnlyFallback = false
153	        do {
154	            // `.automatic` uses the project's iCloud container when an iCloud
155	            // entitlement is present; without one it still loads successfully
156	            // and behaves as a local-only store.
157	            let cloudConfiguration = ModelConfiguration(schema: schema, cloudKitDatabase: .automatic)
158	            container = try ModelContainer(for: schema, configurations: cloudConfiguration)
159	        } catch {
160	            print("ContactManager: CloudKit container failed (\(error.localizedDescription)); using local.")
161	            do {
162	                let localConfiguration = ModelConfiguration(schema: schema, cloudKitDatabase: .none)
163	                container = try ModelContainer(for: schema, configurations: localConfiguration)
164	                isLocalOnlyFallback = true
165	            } catch {
166	                return .failed(error.localizedDescription)
167	            }
168	        }
169	
170	        // Only seed sample data on the confirmed local-only fallback. On a
171	        // CloudKit-capable container the user's real contacts might be about
172	        // to sync down (a fresh install on a second device); seeding then
173	        // would race the sync and propagate the samples to every device.
174	        if isLocalOnlyFallback {
175	            do {
176	                try SampleData.seedIfNeeded(container.mainContext)
177	            } catch {
178	                print("ContactManager: sample data seeding skipped — \(error)")
179	            }
180	        }
181	        // Publish the container for App Intents (Shortcuts/Spotlight queries)
182	        // and kick off an initial Spotlight reindex.
183	        EntityModelContainer.shared = container
184	        Task.detached { await SpotlightIndexer.shared.reindex() }
185	        return .ready(container)

---
name: contactmanager-modernization
description: Direction and PR roadmap for modernizing the ContactManager macOS app
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: a22e27bd-7ce5-4bdb-b1b8-083a9eedbb19
---

The ContactManager app (an old ObjC/AppKit + Core Data + XIB demo) is being rebuilt into a **native macOS Tahoe (macOS 26) app**. Decisions confirmed by the user on 2026-05-25:

- **Full SwiftUI rewrite** (NavigationSplitView: sidebar / list / detail, Liquid Glass).
- **SwiftData** as the persistence layer (replacing Core Data).
- Wants **all four feature areas**: search & alphabetical sections, contact photos (initials fallback), richer fields (multiple emails/phones, company/title, address, birthday, notes), groups/tags + vCard import/export.

**Why:** User wants the app to keep evolving toward a polished native Tahoe contact manager; it doubles as a best-practices demo.

**How to apply:** Ship as focused, single-purpose PRs (matching the existing PR #1–#8 workflow), each building cleanly. Roadmap + status:
- PR A — Foundation ✅ MERGED — GitHub PR #9. SwiftUI+SwiftData shell, CRUD, seed data, macOS 26, ObjC/XIB removed, Swift Testing.
- PR B — Richer fields ✅ MERGED — GitHub PR #11 (originally #10; see lesson below). ContactField child entity (labeled emails/phones), company/title/address/birthday/notes, grouped Form editor.
- PR C — Search & sections ✅ MERGED — GitHub PR #12. Live search, alphabetical sections + "#", Last/First name sort toggle (@AppStorage).
- PR D — Contact photos ✅ MERGED — GitHub PR #13. External-storage photoData, photo well in detail, ImageProcessing downscaling, off-main processing.
- PR E — Groups & vCard ✅ MERGED — GitHub PR #14. ContactGroup many-to-many, sidebar group CRUD, detail membership toggles, pure vCard 3.0 reader/writer + File import/export.
- PR F — Tahoe Liquid Glass polish ✅ MERGED — GitHub PR #15. Glass-prominent action buttons, list/selection/avatar animations, min window size.

Tooling/conventions: PR #16 (`chore/lint-format-and-conventions`) adds SwiftLint + SwiftFormat (configs `.swiftlint.yml`/`.swiftformat`), a Makefile, a SwiftLint build phase, and a root `CLAUDE.md`. Run `make check` (format-check + lint + test) before opening PRs; conventions live in CLAUDE.md (loaded each session). SwiftFormat owns comma/brace style; SwiftLint's trailing_comma is disabled and wrapMultilineStatementBraces is disabled to keep the two consistent + idempotent.

ROADMAP COMPLETE as of 2026-05-26: all six PRs (#9, #11, #12, #13, #14, #15) merged to main. App is a native SwiftUI + SwiftData macOS 26 contact manager; 21 Swift Testing tests across 3 suites green on main. Future work would be net-new (e.g., CloudKit sync, contact dedup, vCard PHOTO round-trip, AppKit→SwiftUI inspector).

Liquid Glass note (macOS 26.5 SDK): `.glassEffect()` modifier and `GlassEffectContainer` are NOT in the SwiftUI swiftinterface, but `.buttonStyle(.glass)` / `.buttonStyle(.glassProminent)` (GlassButtonStyle/GlassProminentButtonStyle) and `.backgroundExtensionEffect()` ARE. Toolbars + NavigationSplitView adopt Liquid Glass automatically when built against the SDK.

As of 2026-05-25, #9 and #11 are MERGED into main; 9 Swift tests green on main.

Copilot review fixes already applied (don't regress): error-state container load with "Reset Data" (no fatalError), save-error alerts + rollback in ContentView, throwing SampleData.seedIfNeeded, deterministic avatar color from createdAt (not hashValue/abs), ContactField sortIndex = max+1.

Key learnings:
- Toolchain: Xcode 26.5, macOS 26.5 SDK, Swift 6.3 (SWIFT_VERSION 5.0 in project). Project `.pbxproj` restructured via the Ruby `xcodeproj` gem (available), not hand-edited. App icon from PR #7 preserved.
- SwiftData tests: use `@Suite(.serialized)` + `@MainActor`, hold the `ModelContainer` as a stored property, and have the host app skip building its own container while testing (`XCTestConfigurationFilePath` env) — concurrent/duplicate containers for the same `@Model` trap SwiftData.
- SwiftData migration: new non-optional scalar attributes MUST have inline defaults (e.g. `var city: String = ""`) or in-place migration fails with NSCocoaError 134110.
- No display access in this environment — cannot screencapture the running app; verify via build + tests + headless launch (process stays alive).
- `gh pr merge --delete-branch` deletes the LOCAL branch too, and deleting a base branch of a stacked PR CLOSES the dependent PR (GitHub won't retarget, and a closed PR whose base is gone can't be reopened/retargeted). For stacked PRs: merge the base PR, then retarget the child PR to main BEFORE deleting the base branch — or just recreate the child PR from its branch (that's what happened: #10 closed → recreated as #11).

content.text:
I have enough to write a thorough, prioritized punch list.

# ContactManager — Pre-ship Punch List

A 100% SwiftUI/SwiftData app with admirably tight architecture (pure helpers, `ContactStore` mutate-with-undo, CloudKit-ready schema, App Intents + Spotlight, in-memory test container). The gaps below are what a senior Apple engineer would still want fixed before calling it "done" on macOS 26.

## P0 — must-fix before shipping

- **App Sandbox is disabled.** `project.pbxproj` has `ENABLE_HARDENED_RUNTIME=YES` but no `ENABLE_APP_SANDBOX=YES`, no `.entitlements` file, and `CODE_SIGN_ENTITLEMENTS` is unset. The app cannot ship on the Mac App Store and won't notarize cleanly. **Fix:** add `ContactManager.entitlements` with `com.apple.security.app-sandbox`, `files.user-selected.read-write` (for `.fileImporter`/`.fileExporter` and drop-import), and (if iCloud is intended) `com.apple.developer.icloud-container-identifiers` + CloudKit service. Evidence: `ContactManager.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj:618,656` (hardened-runtime only).

- **No CloudKit entitlement actually wired.** `ContactManagerApp.swift:141` builds with `cloudKitDatabase: .automatic` but the pbxproj has no `com.apple.developer.icloud-container-identifiers` / `ubiquity-kvstore-identifier`. On a real build the "automatic" path silently degrades to local; the README promises iCloud sync. **Fix:** add the iCloud capability and an entitlements file, or change the README + code comment to say "local-only ship, iCloud parked."

- **`@AppStorage("contactSortOrder")` stores an enum directly.** `ContentView.swift:29`, `SettingsView.swift:14` — `ContactSortOrder` is `String`-rawValue but isn't `RawRepresentable` to `AppStorage` without a manual init; this currently compiles only because of the implicit `RawRepresentable` AppStorage overload available since iOS 17 — verify on a fresh launch with no defaults written. **Fix:** explicit unit test that the default round-trips after a relaunch, or persist `rawValue: String` and map.

- **Unbounded reads on every search keystroke.** `ContactQuery.filtered` (`ContactQuery.swift:49-63`) walks every contact and every field on every keystroke; `searchable` does not debounce. With 10k contacts and a few emails each this will visibly hitch. **Fix:** debounce `searchText` via `.task(id: searchText)` + `try await Task.sleep(for: .milliseconds(150))`, and prefilter by name with a SwiftData `#Predicate` `FetchDescriptor` before the in-memory pass.

- **Spotlight reindex is full-snapshot on every save.** `SpotlightIndexer.reindex()` (`SpotlightIndexer.swift:28-46`) deletes the entire domain and re-adds all contacts on every `contactsDidChange`. At 10k contacts this becomes O(n) CPU per single-field edit. **Fix:** index incrementally — pass the changed contact's id into the notification and call `indexSearchableItems([item])` / `deleteSearchableItems(withIdentifiers:)`. Keep the full reindex as a launch-time path only.

## P1 — should-fix before 1.0

- **No `tel:` / `mailto:` integration.** `ContactDetailView.quickRow` (`ContactDetailView.swift:160-185`) only offers Copy. The macOS-native expectation is a click-to-call/mail action. **Fix:** wrap the value in a `Link` or `Button` calling `NSWorkspace.shared.open(URL(string: "mailto:\(value)")!)`, gated by `force_unwrapping` lint.

- **No ShareLink / Services menu integration.** Nowhere in `Views/` is `ShareLink` or `NSSharingServicePicker` used. **Fix:** add `ShareLink(item: VCardTransfer(...))` to the detail toolbar — reuses the existing `Transferable` and is one line.

- **No keyboard shortcut to focus search.** `searchable` on `ContactListView` (`ContactListView.swift:46`) has no companion `.keyboardShortcut`/`searchFocused`. Cmd-F is the convention. **Fix:** add `@FocusState` with `.searchable(...).searchFocused($searchFocused)` and a `CommandGroup(after: .textEditing)` button bound to ⌘F.

- **No default focus on detail form.** Pressing ⌘N opens a new contact and the user has to mouse to "First Name". **Fix:** `@FocusState` on the first text field, `.defaultFocus($focus, .firstName)` keyed on `contact.persistentModelID`.

- **Birthday assumes `Calendar.current.gregorian`.** `ContactsBridge.swift:85` hardcodes `Calendar(identifier: .gregorian)`; `VCard.swift:55` uses `.autoupdatingCurrent` timezone for a wall-clock date. A user in Tokyo importing a US-exported vCard will routinely see the day shift. **Fix:** store the birthday as `DateComponents` (year/month/day) in a new attribute, migrate the legacy `Date`. Or at minimum lock VCard's serializer/deserializer to UTC noon to make round-trips stable.

- **Photo file read isn't size-limited.** `ContactDetailView.swift:259` does `Data(contentsOf: url)` for any picked file before downscaling. Dropping a 300 MB raw image will allocate 300 MB. **Fix:** prefer `ImageProcessing.avatarData(from: url)` (`ImageProcessing.swift:22-25`) — it already exists and uses `CGImageSourceCreateWithURL` so the full file never enters memory.

- **vCard 4.0 not advertised on write.** `VCard.swift:67` always emits `VERSION:3.0`. The Apple Contacts.app on macOS 26 reads 4.0 by default. Round-tripping works, but tagging it 3.0 forfeits the cleaner photo data-URI we already accept on read (`VCard.swift:226`). **Fix:** keep 3.0 (compat-wide) or move to 4.0 + `PHOTO:data:image/jpeg;base64,...` — pick one, document why.

- **System Contacts import fetches everything synchronously into RAM.** `ContactsBridge.swift:46-56` builds an unbounded `[ParsedContact]` array, then `ContactStore.importContacts` (`ContactStore.swift:209-215`) inserts them all in a single undo group and one save. With a 20k-row Address Book this blocks the main actor for seconds (insert is `@MainActor`) and produces a giant single-undo step. **Fix:** chunk the insert (e.g. 500 contacts/save) and show a progress sheet.

- **No progress UI for long imports.** All three import paths (`ContentView+Import.swift:24-155`) show nothing while parsing/inserting. A 5 MB vCard freezes the UI silently. **Fix:** a sheet with a determinate `ProgressView` or an indeterminate `ContentUnavailableView` overlay.

- **vCard 4.0 BDAY `--MM-DD` (no year) is dropped silently.** `VCard.swift:197` slices `prefix(10)` then runs the `yyyy-MM-dd` formatter; `--12-25` fails. **Fix:** add a fallback `MM-dd` formatter; persist as nil-year `DateComponents` (see birthday note above).

- **Group reorder + drag-into-group are missing.** `SidebarView.swift:37-52` is a flat List with no drop destination for `Contact` — yet the row exposes `.draggable`. Dragging from list to sidebar group does nothing. **Fix:** `.dropDestination(for: ContactTransfer.self)` on the group row; route through `store.setMembership`.

- **No Print / Export PDF.** macOS users expect File ▸ Print of a contact card or list. Nothing implements it. **Fix:** `PrintableContact` `View` + `NSPrintOperation` from a menu command.

- **No accessibility label on `.badge(count)` rows.** `SidebarView.swift:33` and `:44` — VoiceOver reads "All Contacts, 3" but doesn't say "contacts." `.accessibilityValue(Text("^[\(count) contact](inflect: true)"))` would. **Fix:** add inflected `.accessibilityValue` and `.accessibilityLabel` overrides on the rows with badges.

## P2 — polish & follow-ups

- **No localization.** Every UI string in Views/ is a Swift string literal; no `.xcstrings`, no `String(localized:)`, no `LocalizationCatalog` resource. `Localizable.xcstrings` should be generated and at least the menu strings extracted. Most LocalizedStringResource use in App Intents (`FindContactIntent.swift:13`) is the only correct example.

- **`abs`-style trap on `Int.min` is correctly avoided in `AvatarView.swift:55`** — good — but `Contact.colorSeed` (`Contact.swift:118`) truncates a Double's bit pattern, so the seed isn't actually stable across the migration of `createdAt` if its sub-ms component changes (not currently possible, but document the assumption).

- **`ContactStore` mutation may fire `contactsDidChange` after a partial undo group.** `ContactStore.swift:262-263` posts inside `mutate` after `save()` — fine, but the catch branch calls `context.rollback()` then `endUndoGrouping()` *without* `setActionName`. SwiftUI may then merge this empty group with the next mutation. **Fix:** explicitly `removeAllActions(withTarget:)` or wrap the failure case in `disableUndoRegistration()`.

- **Detail view loses scroll position on selection swap.** `ContentView.swift:106-107` uses `.id(selectedContact.persistentModelID)` which forces full re-creation — intended for state isolation but kills any in-progress edit animation. Acceptable; just note it.

- **`ContactWindowView` does not auto-close when its contact is deleted.** `ContactWindowView.swift:35-49` shows a "Contact Not Found" placeholder forever. **Fix:** observe `.contactsDidChange` and call `dismissWindow(id: "contact", value: encodedID)` when the contact disappears.

- **No Handoff `NSUserActivity` advertising the open contact.** Only inbound `onContinueUserActivity` from Spotlight is wired (`ContentView.swift:156`). Continuity-style "pick up on another device" is missing — `Contact` already has a stable encoded id. **Fix:** add `.userActivity("com.scottdensmore.ContactManager.viewContact") { activity in ... }` on the detail.

- **`@AppStorage("defaultGroupID")` stores a JSON-encoded `PersistentIdentifier`.** Works, but if the user re-installs the app onto the same store the PID may decode but point at a different store URI in some SwiftData migration paths. **Fix:** guarded by `pruneStaleDefaultGroup` (`SettingsView.swift:61-71`) — keep, but add a unit test that exercises the stale-group prune (`SettingsViewTests` doesn't exist).

- **`fields` order isn't transactionally safe across undo.** `ContactStore.addField` (`ContactStore.swift:55-63`) computes `nextIndex` from in-memory state; if two add operations race in the same RunLoop tick, both could pick the same index. Unlikely under the current main-actor model, but worth a test.

- **No `MenuBarExtra` or Spotlight-like quick-add window.** Optional but a real "feature gap" vs Contacts.app: ⌃⌥Space-style quick contact add.

- **Test coverage gaps:** `ContentView` import flows (`ContentView+Import.swift`) — no test; `SpotlightIndexer` — no test (would need a stub of `CSSearchableIndex`); `SettingsView.pruneStaleDefaultGroup` — no test; `OpenContactIntent` — no test; window-restoration round-trip via `WindowGroup(for: String.self)` — no test. UI tests are intentionally smoke-only (`ContactManagerUITests.swift:14-22`); add at least one journey test (create → edit → delete) so refactors of the form don't go undetected.

- **README is stale.** `README.md:32-35` doesn't mention `Intents/`, App Intents, Spotlight, drag-and-drop, CSV import, the detached window, or `ContactManagerUITests/`. Update the source-layout block and Features list to reflect the last six PRs.

## Things this audit did NOT find missing

- Threading / actor hygiene: image decode is detached, parse is detached, indexer is an `actor`, intent queries snapshot on a fresh `ModelContext` — all correct.
- SwiftData migration safety: every non-optional attribute has an inline default, all to-many relationships are `= []`, no `@Attribute(.unique)`, no `.deny` rules.
- The duplicate-finder logic (`DuplicateFinder.swift`) and merge semantics (`ContactStore.merge`, `mergeFields`) are solid and well-tested.
- VoiceOver hygiene on rows and quick-info is genuinely thoughtful (`ContactListView.swift:148`, `ContactDetailView.swift:174-184`, `AvatarView.swift:44`) — better than most ship-1.0 macOS apps.

Key files referenced: `/Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/contactmanager/ContactManager/App/ContactManagerApp.swift`, `/Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/contactmanager/ContactManager.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj`, `/Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/contactmanager/ContactManager/Models/ContactQuery.swift`, `/Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/contactmanager/ContactManager/Support/SpotlightIndexer.swift`, `/Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/contactmanager/ContactManager/Views/ContactDetailView.swift`, `/Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/contactmanager/ContactManager/Support/VCard.swift`, `/Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/contactmanager/ContactManager/Support/ContactsBridge.swift`, `/Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/contactmanager/README.md`.

- **`@AppStorage("contactSortOrder")` stores an enum directly.** `ContentView.swift:29`, `SettingsView.swift:14` — `ContactSortOrder` is `String`-rawValue but isn't `RawRepresentable` to `AppStorage` without a manual init; this currently compiles only because of the implicit `RawRepresentable` AppStorage overload available since iOS 17 — verify on a fresh launch with no defaults written. **Fix:** explicit unit test that the default round-trips after a relaunch, or persist `rawValue: String` and map.

- **Unbounded reads on every search keystroke.** `ContactQuery.filtered` (`ContactQuery.swift:49-63`) walks every contact and every field on every keystroke; `searchable` does not debounce. With 10k contacts and a few emails each this will visibly hitch. **Fix:** debounce `searchText` via `.task(id: searchText)` + `try await Task.sleep(for: .milliseconds(150))`, and prefilter by name with a SwiftData `#Predicate` `FetchDescriptor` before the in-memory pass.

- **Spotlight reindex is full-snapshot on every save.** `SpotlightIndexer.reindex()` (`SpotlightIndexer.swift:28-46`) deletes the entire domain and re-adds all contacts on every `contactsDidChange`. At 10k contacts this becomes O(n) CPU per single-field edit. **Fix:** index incrementally — pass the changed contact's id into the notification and call `indexSearchableItems([item])` / `deleteSearchableItems(withIdentifiers:)`. Keep the full reindex as a launch-time path only.

## P1 — should-fix before 1.0

- **No `tel:` / `mailto:` integration.** `ContactDetailView.quickRow` (`ContactDetailView.swift:160-185`) only offers Copy. The macOS-native expectation is a click-to-call/mail action. **Fix:** wrap the value in a `Link` or `Button` calling `NSWorkspace.shared.open(URL(string: "mailto:\(value)")!)`, gated by `force_unwrapping` lint.

- **No ShareLink / Services menu integration.** Nowhere in `Views/` is `ShareLink` or `NSSharingServicePicker` used. **Fix:** add `ShareLink(item: VCardTransfer(...))` to the detail toolbar — reuses the existing `Transferable` and is one line.

- **No keyboard shortcut to focus search.** `searchable` on `ContactListView` (`ContactListView.swift:46`) has no companion `.keyboardShortcut`/`searchFocused`. Cmd-F is the convention. **Fix:** add `@FocusState` with `.searchable(...).searchFocused($searchFocused)` and a `CommandGroup(after: .textEditing)` button bound to ⌘F.

- **No default focus on detail form.** Pressing ⌘N opens a new contact and the user has to mouse to "First Name". **Fix:** `@FocusState` on the first text field, `.defaultFocus($focus, .firstName)` keyed on `contact.persistentModelID`.

- **Birthday assumes `Calendar.current.gregorian`.** `ContactsBridge.swift:85` hardcodes `Calendar(identifier: .gregorian)`; `VCard.swift:55` uses `.autoupdatingCurrent` timezone for a wall-clock date. A user in Tokyo importing a US-exported vCard will routinely see the day shift. **Fix:** store the birthday as `DateComponents` (year/month/day) in a new attribute, migrate the legacy `Date`. Or at minimum lock VCard's serializer/deserializer to UTC noon to make round-trips stable.

- **Photo file read isn't size-limited.** `ContactDetailView.swift:259` does `Data(contentsOf: url)` for any picked file before downscaling. Dropping a 300 MB raw image will allocate 300 MB. **Fix:** prefer `ImageProcessing.avatarData(from: url)` (`ImageProcessing.swift:22-25`) — it already exists and uses `CGImageSourceCreateWithURL` so the full file never enters memory.

- **vCard 4.0 not advertised on write.** `VCard.swift:67` always emits `VERSION:3.0`. The Apple Contacts.app on macOS 26 reads 4.0 by default. Round-tripping works, but tagging it 3.0 forfeits the cleaner photo data-URI we already accept on read (`VCard.swift:226`). **Fix:** keep 3.0 (compat-wide) or move to 4.0 + `PHOTO:data:image/jpeg;base64,...` — pick one, document why.

- **System Contacts import fetches everything synchronously into RAM.** `ContactsBridge.swift:46-56` builds an unbounded `[ParsedContact]` array, then `ContactStore.importContacts` (`ContactStore.swift:209-215`) inserts them all in a single undo group and one save. With a 20k-row Address Book this blocks the main actor for seconds (insert is `@MainActor`) and produces a giant single-undo step. **Fix:** chunk the insert (e.g. 500 contacts/save) and show a progress sheet.

- **No progress UI for long imports.** All three import paths (`ContentView+Import.swift:24-155`) show nothing while parsing/inserting. A 5 MB vCard freezes the UI silently. **Fix:** a sheet with a determinate `ProgressView` or an indeterminate `ContentUnavailableView` overlay.

- **vCard 4.0 BDAY `--MM-DD` (no year) is dropped silently.** `VCard.swift:197` slices `prefix(10)` then runs the `yyyy-MM-dd` formatter; `--12-25` fails. **Fix:** add a fallback `MM-dd` formatter; persist as nil-year `DateComponents` (see birthday note above).

- **Group reorder + drag-into-group are missing.** `SidebarView.swift:37-52` is a flat List with no drop destination for `Contact` — yet the row exposes `.draggable`. Dragging from list to sidebar group does nothing. **Fix:** `.dropDestination(for: ContactTransfer.self)` on the group row; route through `store.setMembership`.

- **No Print / Export PDF.** macOS users expect File ▸ Print of a contact card or list. Nothing implements it. **Fix:** `PrintableContact` `View` + `NSPrintOperation` from a menu command.

- **No accessibility label on `.badge(count)` rows.** `SidebarView.swift:33` and `:44` — VoiceOver reads "All Contacts, 3" but doesn't say "contacts." `.accessibilityValue(Text("^[\(count) contact](inflect: true)"))` would. **Fix:** add inflected `.accessibilityValue` and `.accessibilityLabel` overrides on the rows with badges.

## P2 — polish & follow-ups

- **No localization.** Every UI string in Views/ is a Swift string literal; no `.xcstrings`, no `String(localized:)`, no `LocalizationCatalog` resource. `Localizable.xcstrings` should be generated and at least the menu strings extracted. Most LocalizedStringResource use in App Intents (`FindContactIntent.swift:13`) is the only correct example.

- **`abs`-style trap on `Int.min` is correctly avoided in `AvatarView.swift:55`** — good — but `Contact.colorSeed` (`Contact.swift:118`) truncates a Double's bit pattern, so the seed isn't actually stable across the migration of `createdAt` if its sub-ms component changes (not currently possible, but document the assumption).

- **`ContactStore` mutation may fire `contactsDidChange` after a partial undo group.** `ContactStore.swift:262-263` posts inside `mutate` after `save()` — fine, but the catch branch calls `context.rollback()` then `endUndoGrouping()` *without* `setActionName`. SwiftUI may then merge this empty group with the next mutation. **Fix:** explicitly `removeAllActions(withTarget:)` or wrap the failure case in `disableUndoRegistration()`.

- **Detail view loses scroll position on selection swap.** `ContentView.swift:106-107` uses `.id(selectedContact.persistentModelID)` which forces full re-creation — intended for state isolation but kills any in-progress edit animation. Acceptable; just note it.

- **`ContactWindowView` does not auto-close when its contact is deleted.** `ContactWindowView.swift:35-49` shows a "Contact Not Found" placeholder forever. **Fix:** observe `.contactsDidChange` and call `dismissWindow(id: "contact", value: encodedID)` when the contact disappears.

- **No Handoff `NSUserActivity` advertising the open contact.** Only inbound `onContinueUserActivity` from Spotlight is wired (`ContentView.swift:156`). Continuity-style "pick up on another device" is missing — `Contact` already has a stable encoded id. **Fix:** add `.userActivity("com.scottdensmore.ContactManager.viewContact") { activity in ... }` on the detail.

- **`@AppStorage("defaultGroupID")` stores a JSON-encoded `PersistentIdentifier`.** Works, but if the user re-installs the app onto the same store the PID may decode but point at a different store URI in some SwiftData migration paths. **Fix:** guarded by `pruneStaleDefaultGroup` (`SettingsView.swift:61-71`) — keep, but add a unit test that exercises the stale-group prune (`SettingsViewTests` doesn't exist).

- **`fields` order isn't transactionally safe across undo.** `ContactStore.addField` (`ContactStore.swift:55-63`) computes `nextIndex` from in-memory state; if two add operations race in the same RunLoop tick, both could pick the same index. Unlikely under the current main-actor model, but worth a test.

- **No `MenuBarExtra` or Spotlight-like quick-add window.** Optional but a real "feature gap" vs Contacts.app: ⌃⌥Space-style quick contact add.

- **Test coverage gaps:** `ContentView` import flows (`ContentView+Import.swift`) — no test; `SpotlightIndexer` — no test (would need a stub of `CSSearchableIndex`); `SettingsView.pruneStaleDefaultGroup` — no test; `OpenContactIntent` — no test; window-restoration round-trip via `WindowGroup(for: String.self)` — no test. UI tests are intentionally smoke-only (`ContactManagerUITests.swift:14-22`); add at least one journey test (create → edit → delete) so refactors of the form don't go undetected.

- **README is stale.** `README.md:32-35` doesn't mention `Intents/`, App Intents, Spotlight, drag-and-drop, CSV import, the detached window, or `ContactManagerUITests/`. Update the source-layout block and Features list to reflect the last six PRs.

## Things this audit did NOT find missing

- Threading / actor hygiene: image decode is detached, parse is detached, indexer is an `actor`, intent queries snapshot on a fresh `ModelContext` — all correct.
- SwiftData migration safety: every non-optional attribute has an inline default, all to-many relationships are `= []`, no `@Attribute(.unique)`, no `.deny` rules.
- The duplicate-finder logic (`DuplicateFinder.swift`) and merge semantics (`ContactStore.merge`, `mergeFields`) are solid and well-tested.
- VoiceOver hygiene on rows and quick-info is genuinely thoughtful (`ContactListView.swift:148`, `ContactDetailView.swift:174-184`, `AvatarView.swift:44`) — better than most ship-1.0 macOS apps.

Key files referenced: `/Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/contactmanager/ContactManager/App/ContactManagerApp.swift`, `/Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/contactmanager/ContactManager.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj`, `/Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/contactmanager/ContactManager/Models/ContactQuery.swift`, `/Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/contactmanager/ContactManager/Support/SpotlightIndexer.swift`, `/Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/contactmanager/ContactManager/Views/ContactDetailView.swift`, `/Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/contactmanager/ContactManager/Support/VCard.swift`, `/Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/contactmanager/ContactManager/Support/ContactsBridge.swift`, `/Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/contactmanager/README.md`.

Audit complete. The architecture is in good shape — what's missing is ship-readiness and polish, not core features. Here's the prioritized punch list with file:line evidence.

## P0 — must-fix before shipping

1. **App Sandbox is off.** `ENABLE_HARDENED_RUNTIME=YES` but no `ENABLE_APP_SANDBOX`, no `.entitlements` file. Can't notarize cleanly or hit the Mac App Store. → Add `ContactManager.entitlements` with `app-sandbox` + `files.user-selected.read-write`. (`project.pbxproj:618,656`)

2. **CloudKit promised but not wired.** `ContactManagerApp.swift:141` uses `cloudKitDatabase: .automatic`, but the project has no iCloud capability — silently degrades to local. Either add the capability + container ID, or update the README/comment to "local-only ship."

3. **Search hitches at scale.** `ContactQuery.filtered` walks every contact × every field on every keystroke; no debounce. → Add a 150 ms debounce via `.task(id: searchText)` and prefilter with a SwiftData `#Predicate` before the in-memory pass. (`ContactQuery.swift:49-63`)

4. **Spotlight full reindex on every save.** `SpotlightIndexer.reindex()` deletes + re-adds the entire domain per `contactsDidChange`. O(n) CPU per field edit. → Incremental: pass the changed id and call `indexSearchableItems([item])` / `deleteSearchableItems(withIdentifiers:)`. Keep full reindex for launch only. (`SpotlightIndexer.swift:28-46`)

## P1 — before 1.0

- **`mailto:` / `tel:` actions.** Detail rows only Copy; missing the macOS-native click-to-mail/call. (`ContactDetailView.swift:160-185`)
- **`ShareLink` integration.** `VCardTransfer` (already `Transferable`) drops into a one-line `ShareLink` for the detail toolbar.
- **⌘F to focus search.** `searchable` has no `searchFocused`/keyboard shortcut. (`ContactListView.swift:46`)
- **Default focus on first field for new contact.** ⌘N opens the form, you have to mouse to "First Name."
- **Photo read isn't size-limited.** `Data(contentsOf:)` before downscale — drop a 300 MB raw, allocate 300 MB. `ImageProcessing.avatarData(from:)` already does the safe path; use it. (`ContactDetailView.swift:259`)
- **Birthday timezone bug.** `--MM-DD` (vCard 4.0, no year) is dropped silently; current import uses `.autoupdatingCurrent`, so Tokyo→US round-trips can shift a day. Store as `DateComponents`. (`VCard.swift:55,197`, `ContactsBridge.swift:85`)
- **Sidebar groups have no drop destination.** Rows are `.draggable` from the list, but dragging a contact onto a group does nothing.
- **No import progress UI.** All three import paths show nothing — a 5 MB vCard freezes the UI silently. (`ContentView+Import.swift:24-155`)
- **System Contacts import has no chunking.** Builds an unbounded array on main, single mega-undo step. Chunk ~500 per save with progress. (`ContactsBridge.swift:46-56`, `ContactStore.swift:209-215`)
- **No Print / Export PDF.** macOS users expect File ▸ Print of a card or list.

## P2 — polish & follow-ups

- **No localization.** All UI strings are literal; the only correct `LocalizedStringResource` is in `FindContactIntent.swift:13`. Generate `Localizable.xcstrings`.
- **`ContactWindowView` doesn't close when its contact is deleted** — shows "Contact Not Found" forever. (`ContactWindowView.swift:35-49`)
- **No outbound Handoff `NSUserActivity`** for the open contact (inbound from Spotlight is wired).
- **Test gaps:** `ContentView+Import`, `SpotlightIndexer`, `SettingsView.pruneStaleDefaultGroup`, `OpenContactIntent`, window-restoration round-trip. UI tests are intentionally smoke-only — add one create→edit→delete journey.
- **README is stale** — doesn't mention `Intents/`, App Intents, Spotlight, drag-and-drop, CSV import, detached window, or UI tests.

## What the audit cleared

Threading/actor hygiene, SwiftData migration safety, duplicate-finder logic, and VoiceOver hygiene on rows + avatar are all genuinely solid — better than most shipping 1.0 macOS apps.

---

If you want to start somewhere, **P0 #1 (sandbox/entitlements)** is the lowest-effort highest-value item — it unblocks distribution and is essentially a config change. Want me to open a PR for that?

content.text:
# Fewer Permission Prompts

Look through my transcripts' MCP and bash tool calls, and based on those, make a prioritized list of patterns that I should add to my permission allowlist to reduce permission prompts. Focus on read-only commands.

The format for permissions is: `Bash(foo*)`, `Bash(foo)`, `Bash(foo bar *)`, `mcp__slack__slack_read_thread`, etc.

Then, add these to the project `.claude/settings.json` under `permissions.allow`.

## Steps

1. **Locate transcripts.** Session transcripts live at `~/.claude/projects/<sanitized-cwd>/*.jsonl`. Each line is a JSON object. Tool calls appear as `assistant` messages with `message.content[]` entries of `type: "tool_use"`. The `name` field identifies the tool (e.g. `"Bash"`, `"mcp__slack__slack_read_thread"`); for Bash, `input.command` is the shell string.

   Scan the recent transcripts across the user's projects dir — not just the current project — so the allowlist reflects their actual usage. Cap the scan at a reasonable number of recent sessions (e.g. 50 most-recently-modified JSONL files) so this stays fast.

2. **Extract tool-call frequencies.**
   - For `Bash` calls: parse `input.command`, take the leading command token (handling `sudo`, `timeout`, pipes, `&&`, env-var prefixes). Record the command + first subcommand pair (e.g. `git status`, `gh pr view`, `ls`, `cat`).
   - For MCP calls: record the full tool name (e.g. `mcp__slack__slack_read_thread`).
   - Count occurrences across the scanned transcripts.

3. **Filter to read-only.** Keep only commands that don't mutate state. Examples of read-only: `ls`, `cat`, `pwd`, `git status`, `git log`, `git diff`, `git show`, `git branch`, `rg`, `grep`, `find`, `head`, `tail`, `wc`, `file`, `which`, `echo`, `date`, `gh pr view`, `gh pr list`, `gh pr diff`, `gh issue view`, `gh issue list`, `gh run list`, `gh run view`, `gh api` (GET), `bun run typecheck`, `bun run lint`, `bun run test` (for tests that don't mutate), `docker ps`, `docker logs`, `kubectl get`, `kubectl describe`, `ps`, `top`, `df`, `du`, `env`, `printenv`, any MCP tool with `read`/`get`/`list`/`search`/`view` in its name.

   Drop anything that writes, deletes, renames, pushes, merges, installs, or runs a build/test that has side effects. When in doubt, leave it out.

   **Never allowlist a pattern that grants arbitrary code execution.** A wildcard rule for any of these (e.g. `Bash(python3:*)`) is equivalent to allowing arbitrary code execution. This list is not exhaustive — apply the same rule to anything in the same category:
   - Interpreters: `python`/`python3`, `node`, `bun`, `deno`, `ruby`, `perl`, `php`, `lua`, etc.
   - Shells: `bash`, `sh`, `zsh`, `fish`, `eval`, `exec`, `ssh`, etc.
   - Package runners: `npx`, `bunx`, `uvx`, `uv run`, etc.
   - Task-runner wildcards: `npm run *`, `yarn run *`, `pnpm run *`, `bun run *`, `make *`, `just *`, `cargo run *`, `go run *`, etc. — an exact `Bash(bun run typecheck)` is fine, `Bash(bun run *)` is not
   - `gh api *`, `docker run`/`exec`, `kubectl exec`, `sudo`, and similar

4. **Drop commands Claude Code already auto-allows.** These don't need an allowlist entry — they never prompt. If you see any of these in the transcripts, skip them; don't suggest them to the user.

   - **Always auto-allowed (any args):** `cal`, `uptime`, `cat`, `head`, `tail`, `wc`, `stat`, `strings`, `hexdump`, `od`, `nl`, `id`, `uname`, `free`, `df`, `du`, `locale`, `groups`, `nproc`, `basename`, `dirname`, `realpath`, `cut`, `paste`, `tr`, `column`, `tac`, `rev`, `fold`, `expand`, `unexpand`, `fmt`, `comm`, `cmp`, `numfmt`, `readlink`, `diff`, `true`, `false`, `sleep`, `which`, `type`, `expr`, `test`, `getconf`, `seq`, `tsort`, `pr`, `echo`, `printf`, `ls`, `cd`, `find`.
   - **Auto-allowed with zero args only:** `pwd`, `whoami`, `alias`.
   - **Auto-allowed exact forms:** `claude -h`, `claude --help`, `node -v`, `node --version`, `python --version`, `python3 --version`, `ip addr`.
   - **Auto-allowed with safe flags only (validated):** `xargs`, `file`, `sed` (read-only expressions), `sort`, `man`, `help`, `netstat`, `ps`, `base64`, `grep`, `egrep`, `fgrep`, `sha256sum`, `sha1sum`, `md5sum`, `tree`, `date`, `hostname`, `info`, `lsof`, `pgrep`, `tput`, `ss`, `fd`, `fdfind`, `aki`, `rg`, `jq`, `uniq`, `history`, `arch`, `ifconfig`, `pyright`.
   - **All git read-only subcommands:** `git status`, `git log`, `git diff`, `git show`, `git blame`, `git branch`, `git tag`, `git remote`, `git ls-files`, `git ls-remote`, `git config --get`, `git rev-parse`, `git describe`, `git stash list`, `git reflog`, `git shortlog`, `git cat-file`, `git for-each-ref`, `git worktree list`, etc.
   - **All gh read-only subcommands:** `gh pr view`, `gh pr list`, `gh pr diff`, `gh pr checks`, `gh pr status`, `gh issue view`, `gh issue list`, `gh issue status`, `gh run view`, `gh run list`, `gh workflow list`, `gh workflow view`, `gh repo view`, `gh release view`, `gh release list`, `gh api` (GET), `gh auth status`, etc.
   - **Docker read-only subcommands:** `docker ps`, `docker images`, `docker logs`, `docker inspect`.

   Source of truth: `src/tools/BashTool/readOnlyValidation.ts` (`READONLY_COMMANDS`, `READONLY_NOARGS`, `READONLY_EXACT`, `COMMAND_ALLOWLIST`) and `src/utils/shell/readOnlyCommandValidation.ts` (`GIT_READ_ONLY_COMMANDS`, `GH_READ_ONLY_COMMANDS`, `DOCKER_READ_ONLY_COMMANDS`, `RIPGREP_READ_ONLY_COMMANDS`, `PYRIGHT_READ_ONLY_COMMANDS`). If the user is in this repo and you're unsure whether a command is covered, grep these files rather than guessing.

5. **Pick the pattern form.** Use the narrowest pattern that still covers the observed usage:
   - If the user runs many variants (`git log`, `git log --oneline`, `git log main..HEAD`): use `Bash(git log *)` — note the space before `*`, which is required for prefix matching to work correctly.
   - If a single exact invocation is common: use `Bash(foo)` with no wildcard.
   - For MCP: use the full tool name verbatim (no wildcard needed; they're already specific).
   - Never widen a pattern to the point that it conflicts with the rules above (no arbitrary code execution, no mutation/side effects).

6. **Prioritize.** Rank by count descending. Drop anything that appeared fewer than ~3 times — not worth the allowlist entry. Cap the list at the top ~20 so the user can skim it.

7. **Present the prioritized list to the user** as a markdown table with columns: rank, pattern, count, one-line description. Example:

   | # | Pattern | Count | Notes |
   |---|---------|-------|-------|
   | 1 | `Bash(git status *)` | 142 | repo status checks |
   | 2 | `Bash(gh pr view *)` | 87 | PR inspection |
   | 3 | `mcp__slack__slack_read_thread` | 54 | Slack thread reads |

8. **Merge into `.claude/settings.json`** in the current project (not `~/.claude/settings.json`, not `.claude/settings.local.json`). Create the file if it doesn't exist. Preserve existing keys and existing entries in `permissions.allow`; de-duplicate against what's already there; don't remove anything; don't reorder unrelated fields.

9. **Report back.** Tell the user what you added (count + a few examples), what was already in the allowlist, and what you skipped and why (e.g. "dropped `rm` and `git push` — not read-only; dropped `cat`/`ls`/`git status` — already auto-allowed, no rule needed").

Do not add anything to `permissions.deny` or `permissions.ask`. Do not touch any other settings field.
I'll scan recent transcripts across your projects dir, tally bash/MCP tool calls, then propose an allowlist.

#!/usr/bin/env ruby
# Enables App Sandbox on the ContactManager app target and wires up the
# entitlements file. Idempotent — re-running leaves the project unchanged.
require "xcodeproj"

project_path = File.expand_path(
  "../ContactManager.xcodeproj",
  File.expand_path(File.dirname(__FILE__))
)
project = Xcodeproj::Project.open(project_path)

app_target = project.native_targets.find { |t| t.name == "ContactManager" }
abort "ContactManager target not found" unless app_target

entitlements_relative_path = "ContactManager/ContactManager.entitlements"

# Register the file in the project tree (sibling group "ContactManager") so it
# shows up in Xcode's navigator alongside Info.plist-style resources. Add to
# the project but NOT to any build phase — Xcode picks it up via CODE_SIGN_ENTITLEMENTS.
contact_manager_group = project.main_group["ContactManager"] ||
                        project.main_group.find_subpath("ContactManager", true)
unless contact_manager_group.files.any? { |f| f.path == "ContactManager.entitlements" }
  contact_manager_group.new_file("ContactManager.entitlements")
end

app_target.build_configurations.each do |config|
  config.build_settings["CODE_SIGN_ENTITLEMENTS"] = entitlements_relative_path
  config.build_settings["ENABLE_APP_SANDBOX"] = "YES"
end

project.save
puts "Enabled App Sandbox + entitlements on target 'ContactManager'."

# Autonomous loop check

You're being invoked on a timer while the user is away or occupied. The point is to keep work moving forward without the user driving every step — finishing things they started, maintaining PRs they're building, catching problems before they come back to find them. You're a steward, not an initiator. The user set you loose on their work, and the value you provide comes from reliably advancing things they've already set in motion, not from finding new things to do.

The key tension to navigate: the user trusts you enough to run autonomously, but that trust is easily lost. Acting on what the conversation already established is safe and valuable. Inventing new work or making irreversible changes without clear authorization erodes trust fast. When you're unsure whether something falls into "continuing established work" or "inventing new work," lean toward the former only when the transcript provides clear evidence the user wanted it done. If you find yourself reaching for justifications about why a push is probably fine, that's a signal to wait.

## What to act on

The current conversation is your highest-signal source — re-read the transcript above, since everything there is something the user was actively engaged with. The strongest signal is an in-progress PR you've been building together: review comments to address and resolve, failing CI checks to diagnose (and re-enqueue if they're flakes), merge conflicts to fix. The goal is to get the PR into a state where it's ready to merge pending only human review — the user shouldn't come back to find a PR blocked on things you could have handled. After that, look for unfinished implementation where the last exchange left something half-done, and explicit "I'll also..." or "next I'll..." commitments the conversation made and didn't honor. Weaker but still real: dangling questions you could now answer, verification steps that were skipped, edge cases that were mentioned but not handled, and natural continuations that don't require new decisions.

If you find anything in this category, act on it — actually do the work, don't describe what could be done. Run the tests, don't say "you could run the tests." The whole point of autonomous operation is that work gets done while the user is away.

When the conversation transcript has nothing left, the current branch's pull/merge request on the user's SCM is the next-best place to look. This is maintenance work — valuable, but lower priority than continuing the user's active work. Find the PR/MR for the current branch via the SCM's CLI, then check three things: CI status, unresolved review threads, and whether the branch has fallen behind the base. For failing CI, pull the failing job's logs and diagnose before acting — flaky-shaped failures (timeout, runner died, transient network) can be re-enqueued; real failures need a reproduction and a minimal fix. For unresolved review threads, fetch the comment, address the feedback, push, and resolve the thread via, for example, the GitHub GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation (or the equivalent for whichever SCM the project uses). Before pushing anything, check whether someone else has pushed to the branch while you were working — if so, rebase (don't merge) to keep history clean.

When CI is green, threads are clear, and there's idle time, sweeping the branch for issues is a good use of that time — bug-hunt or simplification passes catch problems before reviewers do, saving everyone a round-trip.

If everything is genuinely quiet — no conversation work, no PR maintenance — say so in one sentence and stop. No summary of what you checked, no list of what you might do later. The user will see your message in the transcript when they come back; three consecutive "nothing to do" results means you should scale back to a quick CI check and stop, not narrate.

## Repeated invocations

If you see earlier autonomous checks in this conversation, adjust your scope accordingly. If a previous check left a question the user hasn't answered, the cost of acting depends on reversibility: for reversible actions (local edits, running tests), make your best call and proceed; for irreversible ones (pushing, deleting, sending), keep waiting — the cost of acting wrongly on something irreversible is much higher than the cost of waiting one more cycle. If three or more consecutive checks have found nothing actionable, things are quiet — do one quick CI/threads check and stop in a single line. Repeated "nothing to do" messages clutter the transcript and waste the user's attention when they come back to review.

Read and analyze freely — understanding the state of things has no blast radius. Make edits and run tests when you're confident they continue established work. Commit and push only when you're clearly continuing something the user authorized, or when the work pattern makes the intent obvious — like fixing CI on a PR you've been building together.

## Summary
Two fixes for the search hitch that gets visible past a few thousand contacts (audit P0 #3).

- **Debounce.** `ContentView.searchText` now feeds a `debouncedSearchText` that trails by 150 ms via `.task(id: searchText)`. A burst of keystrokes triggers one filter pass instead of one per character. Empty searches skip the delay so clearing the field returns the full list instantly.
- **Filter optimization.** `ContactQuery.filtered` no longer lowercases every field of every contact and folds them into a temporary array. It now uses `localizedCaseInsensitiveContains` on each candidate in turn, short-circuiting on the cheap scalar attributes (`fullName`, `company`, `jobTitle`, `notes`) before walking the to-many `fields` relationship. No more per-string allocation; the field walk only runs for contacts whose scalars didn't already hit.

Intentionally **not** in this PR: rewriting `@Query` as a child view with a SwiftData `#Predicate` `FetchDescriptor`. The debounce already eliminates the per-keystroke hitch; the predicate refactor is a structurally bigger change (child view with `@Query`, group-scope predicate construction) that's worth handling separately if the in-memory pass starts mattering at larger N.

## Test plan
- [x] `make check` — all 21 unit tests + 3 UI tests pass
- [x] Existing search-semantics coverage in `ContactModelTests` (name, company+email, notes, phone-field match, empty passes all through, no-match returns empty) is unchanged and green
- [ ] Manual: type quickly into the search field with the seeded sample and confirm the list updates after a brief pause rather than per-character
- [ ] Manual: clear the search field and confirm the list snaps back without delay

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

## computer-use
You have a computer-use MCP available (tools named `mcp__computer-use__*`). It lets you take screenshots of the user's desktop and control it with mouse clicks, keyboard input, and scrolling.

**Pick the right tool for the app.** Each tier trades speed/precision against coverage:

1. **Dedicated MCP for the app** — if the task is in an app that has its own MCP (Slack, Gmail, Calendar, Linear, etc.) and that MCP is connected, use it. API-backed tools are fast and precise.
2. **Chrome MCP** (`mcp__claude-in-chrome__*`) — if the target is a web app and there's no dedicated MCP for it, use the browser tools. DOM-aware, much faster than clicking pixels. If the Chrome extension isn't connected, ask the user to install it rather than falling through to computer use.
3. **Computer use** — for native desktop apps (Maps, Notes, Finder, Photos, System Settings, any third-party native app) and cross-app workflows. Computer use IS the right tool here — don't decline a native-app task just because there's no dedicated MCP for it.

This is about what's available, not error handling — if a dedicated MCP tool errors, debug or report it rather than silently retrying via a slower tier.

**Look before you assert.** If the user asks about app state (what's open, what's connected, what an app can do), take a screenshot and check before answering. Don't answer from memory — the user's setup or app version may differ from what you expect. If you're about to say an app doesn't support an action, that claim should be grounded in what you just saw on screen, not general knowledge. Similarly, `list_granted_applications` or a fresh `screenshot` is cheaper than a wrong assertion about what's running.

**Loading via ToolSearch — load in bulk, not one-by-one:** if computer-use tools are in the deferred list, load them ALL in a single ToolSearch call: `{ query: "computer-use", max_results: 30 }`. The keyword search matches the server-name substring in every tool name, so one query returns the entire toolkit. Don't use `select:` for individual tools — that's one round-trip per tool.

**Access flow:** before any computer-use action you must call `request_access` with the list of applications you need. The user approves each application explicitly, and you may need to call it again mid-task if you discover you need another application.

**Tiered apps:** some apps are granted at a restricted tier based on their category — the tier is displayed in the approval dialog and returned in the `request_access` response:
- **Browsers** (Safari, Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Arc, etc.) → tier **"read"**: visible in screenshots, but clicks and typing are blocked. You can read what's already on screen. For navigation, clicking, or form-filling, use the claude-in-chrome MCP (tools named `mcp__claude-in-chrome__*`; load via ToolSearch if deferred).
- **Terminals and IDEs** (Terminal, iTerm, VS Code, JetBrains, etc.) → tier **"click"**: visible and left-clickable, but typing, key presses, right-click, modifier-clicks, and drag-drop are blocked. You can click a Run button or scroll test output, but cannot type into the editor or integrated terminal, cannot right-click (the context menu has Paste), and cannot drag text onto them. For shell commands, use the Bash tool.
- **Everything else** → tier **"full"**: no restrictions.

The tier is enforced by the frontmost-app check: if a tier-"read" app is in front, `left_click` returns an error; if a tier-"click" app is in front, `type` and `right_click` return errors. The error tells you what tier the app has and what to do instead. `open_application` works at any tier — bringing an app forward is a read-level operation.

**Link safety — treat links in emails and messages as suspicious by default.**
- **Never click web links with computer-use tools.** If you encounter a link in a native app (Mail, Messages, a PDF, etc.), do NOT `left_click` it. Open the URL via the claude-in-chrome MCP instead.
- **See the full URL before following any link.** Visible link text can be misleading — hover or inspect to get the real destination.
- **Links from emails, messages, or unknown-sender documents are suspicious by default.** If the destination URL is at all unfamiliar or looks off, ask the user for confirmation before proceeding.
- **Inside the Chrome extension** you can click links with the extension's tools, but the suspicion check still applies — verify unfamiliar URLs with the user.

**Financial actions - do not execute trades or move money.** Budgeting and accounting apps (Quicken, YNAB, QuickBooks, etc.) are granted at full tier so you can categorize transactions, generate reports, and help the user organize their finances. But never execute a trade, place an order, send money, or initiate a transfer on the user's behalf - always ask the user to perform those actions themselves.

1	//
2	//  SpotlightIndexer.swift
3	//  ContactManager
4	//
5	//  Pushes contacts into Spotlight's index. An actor so back-to-back calls
6	//  during a burst of mutations serialize cleanly — a delete-then-index
7	//  pair must not interleave with another pair or Spotlight ends up
8	//  reflecting an older snapshot (or empty).
9	//
10	//  Each call fetches a fresh snapshot via `ContactEntityQuery` instead of
11	//  trusting a caller-supplied list, because `@Query`'s post-save refresh
12	//  is asynchronous and can lag a `ContactStore.mutate` notification.
13	//
14	
15	import CoreSpotlight
16	import Foundation
17	
18	actor SpotlightIndexer {
19	    static let shared = SpotlightIndexer()
20	
21	    /// Identifier scope so we can clear *our* items without touching
22	    /// anything else Spotlight has indexed for this app.
23	    static let domainIdentifier = "com.scottdensmore.ContactManager.contacts"
24	
25	    /// Replaces our entire indexed set with a freshly-fetched snapshot.
26	    /// Errors are logged but not surfaced — a Spotlight indexing failure
27	    /// is a quality-of-life regression, not a user-visible error.
28	    func reindex() async {
29	        let entities = await (try? ContactEntityQuery().allEntities()) ?? []
30	        let index = CSSearchableIndex.default()
31	        let items = entities.map { entity in
32	            CSSearchableItem(
33	                uniqueIdentifier: entity.id,
34	                domainIdentifier: Self.domainIdentifier,
35	                attributeSet: entity.attributeSet
36	            )
37	        }
38	        do {
39	            try await index.deleteSearchableItems(withDomainIdentifiers: [Self.domainIdentifier])
40	            if !items.isEmpty {
41	                try await index.indexSearchableItems(items)
42	            }
43	        } catch {
44	            print("Spotlight reindex failed: \(error)")
45	        }
46	    }
47	}

The ContactManager app (an old ObjC/AppKit + Core Data + XIB demo) is being rebuilt into a **native macOS Tahoe (macOS 26) app**. Decisions confirmed by the user on 2026-05-25:

- **Full SwiftUI rewrite** (NavigationSplitView: sidebar / list / detail, Liquid Glass).
- **SwiftData** as the persistence layer (replacing Core Data).
- Wants **all four feature areas**: search & alphabetical sections, contact photos (initials fallback), richer fields (multiple emails/phones, company/title, address, birthday, notes), groups/tags + vCard import/export.

**Why:** User wants the app to keep evolving toward a polished native Tahoe contact manager; it doubles as a best-practices demo.

**How to apply:** Ship as focused, single-purpose PRs (matching the existing PR #1–#8 workflow), each building cleanly. Roadmap + status:
- PR A — Foundation ✅ MERGED — GitHub PR #9. SwiftUI+SwiftData shell, CRUD, seed data, macOS 26, ObjC/XIB removed, Swift Testing.
- PR B — Richer fields ✅ MERGED — GitHub PR #11 (originally #10; see lesson below). ContactField child entity (labeled emails/phones), company/title/address/birthday/notes, grouped Form editor.
- PR C — Search & sections ✅ MERGED — GitHub PR #12. Live search, alphabetical sections + "#", Last/First name sort toggle (@AppStorage).
- PR D — Contact photos ✅ MERGED — GitHub PR #13. External-storage photoData, photo well in detail, ImageProcessing downscaling, off-main processing.
- PR E — Groups & vCard ✅ MERGED — GitHub PR #14. ContactGroup many-to-many, sidebar group CRUD, detail membership toggles, pure vCard 3.0 reader/writer + File import/export.
- PR F — Tahoe Liquid Glass polish ✅ MERGED — GitHub PR #15. Glass-prominent action buttons, list/selection/avatar animations, min window size.

Tooling/conventions: PR #16 (`chore/lint-format-and-conventions`) adds SwiftLint + SwiftFormat (configs `.swiftlint.yml`/`.swiftformat`), a Makefile, a SwiftLint build phase, and a root `CLAUDE.md`. Run `make check` (format-check + lint + test) before opening PRs; conventions live in CLAUDE.md (loaded each session). SwiftFormat owns comma/brace style; SwiftLint's trailing_comma is disabled and wrapMultilineStatementBraces is disabled to keep the two consistent + idempotent.

ROADMAP COMPLETE as of 2026-05-26: all six PRs (#9, #11, #12, #13, #14, #15) merged to main. App is a native SwiftUI + SwiftData macOS 26 contact manager; 21 Swift Testing tests across 3 suites green on main. Future work would be net-new (e.g., CloudKit sync, contact dedup, vCard PHOTO round-trip, AppKit→SwiftUI inspector).

Liquid Glass note (macOS 26.5 SDK): `.glassEffect()` modifier and `GlassEffectContainer` are NOT in the SwiftUI swiftinterface, but `.buttonStyle(.glass)` / `.buttonStyle(.glassProminent)` (GlassButtonStyle/GlassProminentButtonStyle) and `.backgroundExtensionEffect()` ARE. Toolbars + NavigationSplitView adopt Liquid Glass automatically when built against the SDK.

As of 2026-05-25, #9 and #11 are MERGED into main; 9 Swift tests green on main.

Copilot review fixes already applied (don't regress): error-state container load with "Reset Data" (no fatalError), save-error alerts + rollback in ContentView, throwing SampleData.seedIfNeeded, deterministic avatar color from createdAt (not hashValue/abs), ContactField sortIndex = max+1.

Key learnings:
- Toolchain: Xcode 26.5, macOS 26.5 SDK, Swift 6.3 (SWIFT_VERSION 5.0 in project). Project `.pbxproj` restructured via the Ruby `xcodeproj` gem (available), not hand-edited. App icon from PR #7 preserved.
- SwiftData tests: use `@Suite(.serialized)` + `@MainActor`, hold the `ModelContainer` as a stored property, and have the host app skip building its own container while testing (`XCTestConfigurationFilePath` env) — concurrent/duplicate containers for the same `@Model` trap SwiftData.
- SwiftData migration: new non-optional scalar attributes MUST have inline defaults (e.g. `var city: String = ""`) or in-place migration fails with NSCocoaError 134110.
- No display access in this environment — cannot screencapture the running app; verify via build + tests + headless launch (process stays alive).
- `gh pr merge --delete-branch` deletes the LOCAL branch too, and deleting a base branch of a stacked PR CLOSES the dependent PR (GitHub won't retarget, and a closed PR whose base is gone can't be reopened/retargeted). For stacked PRs: merge the base PR, then retarget the child PR to main BEFORE deleting the base branch — or just recreate the child PR from its branch (that's what happened: #10 closed → recreated as #11).

3	//  ContactManager
4	//
5	//  A process-wide handle to the app's `ModelContainer`. App Intents and
6	//  CoreSpotlight queries need to read contacts without going through the
7	//  SwiftUI environment; the app sets this once after `loadContainer()`
8	//  succeeds and the entity query reads it from any actor.
9	//
10	//  Access is guarded by an `OSAllocatedUnfairLock` so concurrent reads
11	//  during a launch-time setup are well-defined. The lock is held only
12	//  long enough to read or write the container reference itself.
13	//

16	import os
17	import SwiftData
18

20	    private static let storage = OSAllocatedUnfairLock<ModelContainer?>(initialState: nil)
21	
22	    /// The shared container, populated by `ContactManagerApp` after the
23	    /// store loads. `nil` if loading failed or we're running under tests.
24	    static var shared: ModelContainer? {
25	        get { storage.withLock { $0 } }
26	        set { storage.withLock { $0 = newValue } }
27	    }
28	}

1	//
2	//  PersistentIdentifierEncoding.swift
3	//  ContactManager
4	//
5	//  `PersistentIdentifier` is `Codable` but not directly storable in
6	//  `@AppStorage` (which only takes a handful of property-list types).
7	//  These helpers round-trip a PID through a JSON string so a preference
8	//  can point at a SwiftData model without depending on a mutable display
9	//  name.
10	//
11	
12	import Foundation
13	import SwiftData
14	
15	extension PersistentIdentifier {
16	    /// JSON-encoded string suitable for `@AppStorage`. Returns `nil` only
17	    /// if encoding unexpectedly fails (shouldn't in practice).
18	    var storedString: String? {
19	        guard let data = try? JSONEncoder().encode(self) else { return nil }
20	        return String(data: data, encoding: .utf8)
21	    }
22	
23	    /// Inverse of `storedString`. Returns `nil` for the empty string (the
24	    /// "no value" sentinel) or any string that isn't a valid encoded PID.
25	    static func decode(stored string: String) -> PersistentIdentifier? {
26	        guard !string.isEmpty,
27	              let data = string.data(using: .utf8),
28	              let id = try? JSONDecoder().decode(PersistentIdentifier.self, from: data)
29	        else { return nil }
30	        return id
31	    }
32	}

content.text:
Found the real bug — and it's a meaningful one. The failure shows the **same** contact (`p1`, identical URI) encoding to two **different** strings because `storedString` uses `JSONEncoder` without sorted keys, so key order is non-deterministic *within a single process*.

This isn't just a test problem: my incremental `apply(_:)` matches contacts by `storedString` equality in `ContactEntityQuery.entities(for:)` (`wanted.contains($0.persistentModelID.storedString)`). If the id encoded at save-time differs in key order from the id encoded at fetch-time, the lookup misses and the contact silently never gets indexed. The pre-existing Spotlight-open path has the same latent fragility. The fix is to make `storedString` canonical:

content.content: The file /Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/contactmanager/ContactManager/Support/PersistentIdentifierEncoding.swift has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: /// JSON-encoded string suitable for `@AppStorage`. Returns `nil` only
/// if encoding unexpectedly fails (shouldn't in practice).
+    /// Keys are sorted so the *same* identifier always encodes to the *same*
+    /// string. Without this, `PersistentIdentifier`'s Codable output reorders
+    /// keys between encodings (even within one process), and any code that
+    /// compares two separately-encoded ids by string — Spotlight's
+    /// `entities(for:)` lookup, incremental re-index matching — would miss.
-        guard let data = try? JSONEncoder().encode(self) else { return nil }
+        guard let data = try? encoder.encode(self) else { return nil }

Every ContactStore mutation posted a payload-less .contactsDidChange, and
ContentView answered it by rebuilding the *entire* Spotlight index
(delete-the-whole-domain + re-index every contact). That's O(all
contacts) on each single edit, plus a window where the index is briefly
empty.

Now `mutate` snapshots the context's pending changes around the save and
broadcasts a `ContactChange` { updatedIDs, deletedIDs }:
- updated = inserted/changed Contacts, plus the owning Contact of any
  inserted/changed/deleted ContactField (a contact's emails/phones feed
  its Spotlight entry), minus anything being deleted.
- deleted = removed Contacts.
Ids are read *after* the save so a freshly inserted contact carries its
permanent PersistentIdentifier, not the temporary pre-save one that
wouldn't match a later fetch. Owners of deleted fields are captured
*before* the save while the inverse relationship is still intact.

`SpotlightIndexer.apply(_:)` consumes the delta: delete the removed ids,
fetch only the updated contacts by id, re-index those. O(changed) instead
of O(all). `reindex()` stays for launch and as the fallback when a post
arrives without a payload. Group-only changes (e.g. rename) map to no
contact and are a no-op.

Also makes `PersistentIdentifier.storedString` deterministic
(`.sortedKeys`). Its Codable output otherwise reorders keys between
encodings — even within one process — so the id captured at save time
could differ as a string from the same id re-encoded at fetch time,
making the by-id lookup (and the pre-existing Spotlight-open path) miss.
Surfaced by a flaky equality assertion in the new tests.

Tests: new SpotlightDeltaTests suite asserts the emitted delta for
create / delete / add-field / delete-field / group-rename / merge;
ContactEntityTests gains an id-encoding stability check. 101 unit tests
+ 3 UI tests green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
